item.title: Interleukin-8-Driven Monocyte Activation in Primary Sclerosing Cholangitis: Insights from Single-Cell Analysis and Humanized Immune System Mice.
item.description: JHEP reports : innovation in hepatology - Primary sclerosing cholangitis (PSC) is an immune-mediated liver disease with an incompletely understood pathogenesis. Monocyte/macrophage cell dysregulation has been suggested to contribute, altho...

item.title: Nanoarmored Probiotic for Inflammatory Bowel Disease Bacteriotherapy via a Dual-Targeting Host-Microbiota Reprogramming.
item.description: ACS nano - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) involves oxidative stress, chronic inflammation, and gut microbiota dysbiosis, complicating therapeutic intervention. Herein, we engineer a robust living therapeuti...

item.title: Gut Bacteria: A Beneficial Symbiosis or a Hidden Threat? Investigating the Dual Role of Bacteria in Gastrointestinal Diseases.
item.description: Current microbiology - Review - The intestinal microbiota is a highly dynamic and intricate system that supports digestive function, modulates immune responses, and protects against gut disorders. This review explores the impact ...

item.title: Sinomenine alleviates ulcerative colitis by targeting FXR to regulate arachidonic acid metabolism and Th17/Treg homeostasis.
item.description: European journal of pharmacology - Sinomenine (SIN), a bioactive alkaloid with anti-inflammatory activity, has shown therapeutic potential in ulcerative colitis (UC), but its precise molecular targets remain unclear. This study inve...

item.title: Lotus-derived protein-rich nanoparticles relieve inflammatory bowel disease by modulating gut microbiota and bile acid metabolism.
item.description: International journal of biological macromolecules - Plant-derived nanovesicles (PDNs) enriched with diverse bioactive constituents, including proteins, lipids, and RNAs, which possess a wide range of biological activities. PDNs exhibit therapeutic p...

item.title: Shared CD4+ T cell receptor specificity groups in Crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis.
item.description: JCI insight - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), encompassing ulcerative colitis (UC) and Crohn's disease (CD), is marked by chronic intestinal inflammation and dysregulated immunity. Although UC and CD affect di...

item.title: Metagenomic insights into potential horizontal transfer of resistance/virulence genes in gut microbiota from patients with Crohn disease.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Unraveling the potential horizontal transfer of resistance genes/virulence genes (RGs/VGs) in gut microbiota from patients with Crohn disease (CD) is an interesting but poorly characterized issue. ...

item.title: Polyphenols as next-generation prebiotics targeting intestinal mucosal tolerance.
item.description: Biomedicine & pharmacotherapy = Biomedecine & pharmacotherapie - Review - Polyphenols, long recognized for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, are widely consumed as dietary components or as constituents of functional foods and nutraceuticals. Emerging ev...

item.title: Possible Gastroenterological Causes of FUO (Fever of Unknown Origin).
item.description: Journal of clinical medicine - Review - Fever of unknown origin (FUO) remains a persistent diagnostic challenge in clinical medicine despite significant advances in laboratory testing and imaging techniques. The definition of FUO has evo...

item.title: Interleukin-10 Autoantibodies and HLA-DRB1*01:03 in Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: The New England journal of medicine - Neutralizing autoantibodies against interleukin-10 can result in a phenocopy of monogenic defects of interleukin-10 signaling in children and may be associated with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD)...

item.title: Remodeling of capsular polysaccharide enhances colonization and therapeutic efficacy of probiotic Escherichia coli against colitis.
item.description: Biomaterials - Engineered bacterial therapeutics have shown promising performance in treating various chronic and inflammatory diseases. However, inadequate colonization of therapeutic bacteria at disease sites, ...

item.title: Inonotus obliquus Crude Melanin Ameliorates DSS-Induced Colitis with Modulation of Gut Microbiota and Neutrophil pro-NETotic Activation.
item.description: Nutrients - Background: Inonotus obliquus, a fungus known for its edible and medicinal properties, has traditionally been used as a herbal tea to relieve gastrointestinal disorders. Melanin, a major active con...

item.title: Colon-targeted alginate/chitosan microspheres for co-delivery of synbiotics and nanozymes ameliorate inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Food research international (Ottawa, Ont.) - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is closely associated with gut dysbiosis and immune imbalance. Synbiotics, combining probiotics and prebiotics, represent a promising dietary strategy; however, the...

item.title: A Review on the Molecular Dynamics of Enterotype Bacteroides 2 in Relation to Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: International journal of molecular sciences - Review - Bacteroides 2 (Bact2) is a dysbiotic enterotype often associated with susceptibility to developing diseases such as inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). Carriers of Bact2 are found to be less responsi...

item.title: Pro-Inflammatory Cytokines as Core Mediators of Colonic Epithelial Barrier Dysfunction: Roles of TNF-α, IFN-γ, IL-1β, and IL-6.
item.description: International journal of molecular sciences - Review - The colonic epithelial barrier is a multilayered defense system comprising the mucus layer, intestinal epithelial cells (IECs), and the underlying lamina propria. These components collectively main...
